summary:
Claim: Immediate surgical intervention on the affected side and prophylactic surgery on the contralateral side is necessary to preserve fertility and prevent recurrent torsion.  
Evidence: Testicular torsion in children often affects both sides; up to 5-10% of cases involve spontaneous torsion of the contralateral testicle after initial event. Prolonged delay leads to irreversible testicular damage due to ischemia.  
Answer: C
